---
title: "About"
body-classes: "ed-page"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    code-tools: false
---

```{=html}
<main class="ed">

<!-- Hero -->
<section class="l-hero">
  <div class="l-wrap">
    <p class="l-eyebrow">About</p>
    <h1>Epsilon Labs is a statistical consulting practice focused on rigorous, decision-grade analytics.</h1>
    <p class="l-lede">
      The practice serves organizations that need careful statistical
      work to answer questions, support decisions, or ship analytical
      tools their teams can rely on.
    </p>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- Practice -->
<section class="l-section first">
  <div class="l-wrap">
    <p class="l-eyebrow">Practice</p>
    <h2>What we believe</h2>
    <p>
      Most analytics work is judged by how it reads. Epsilon Labs is
      built around the opposite premise: an analysis should be judged
      by how well it holds up under audit, replication, and a critical
      reader. We document assumptions, validate models the right way,
      and hand off code that your team can actually rerun.
    </p>
    <p>
      We work on bounded projects and short retainers. Every engagement
      starts with a scoping conversation and a written proposal. Nothing
      gets billed without a deliverable behind it.
    </p>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- Principal -->
<section class="l-section">
  <div class="l-wrap">
    <p class="l-eyebrow">Principal</p>
    <div class="l-hero-row">
      <div>
        <h2 style="margin-bottom:0.5rem;">Brian Cervantes Alvarez</h2>
        <p>
          Brian is a statistician and data scientist with formal training
          across mathematics, applied data science, and statistics, and
          working experience that spans interactive analytics, predictive
          modeling, and statistical education.
        </p>
        <p>
          He currently serves as a Business Intelligence Analyst at the
          Oregon Department of Education, where he works on educational
          data systems and reporting. Epsilon Labs is operated
          independently of that role.
        </p>
        <div class="l-cta-row">
          <a href="mailto:briancervantesalvarez@gmail.com" class="l-btn">Email</a>
          <a href="https://linkedin.com/in/bcervantesalvarez" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="l-btn l-btn--ghost">LinkedIn</a>
          <a href="/documents/resume/bca-resume.pdf" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="l-btn l-btn--ghost">CV (PDF)</a>
        </div>
      </div>
      <img src="/images/profile.jpg" alt="Brian Cervantes Alvarez, Principal at Epsilon Labs"
           class="l-portrait" loading="eager">
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- Training -->
<section class="l-section">
  <div class="l-wrap">
    <p class="l-eyebrow">Training</p>
    <h2>Where the practice trained</h2>
    <ul class="l-list">
      <li>
        <div class="l-list-label">M.S. Statistics</div>
        <div class="l-list-body">Oregon State University — Bayesian methods, experimental design, and applied modeling. Taught undergraduate sections and developed data-visualization curriculum.</div>
      </li>
      <li>
        <div class="l-list-label">M.S. Data Science</div>
        <div class="l-list-body">Willamette University — Machine learning, predictive modeling, and reproducible analytics workflows.</div>
      </li>
      <li>
        <div class="l-list-label">B.S. Mathematics</div>
        <div class="l-list-body">Linfield University — Calculus, linear algebra, statistics, and the analytical foundations everything sits on.</div>
      </li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- Capabilities -->
<section class="l-section">
  <div class="l-wrap">
    <p class="l-eyebrow">Capabilities</p>
    <h2>What we bring to an engagement</h2>
    <div class="l-cols">
      <div>
        <h3>Statistical analysis</h3>
        <p>Time-to-event analysis, spatial statistics, simulations, and rigorous hypothesis testing.</p>
      </div>
      <div>
        <h3>Predictive modeling</h3>
        <p>Random forests, SVMs, gradient boosting, and neural networks — with an emphasis on validation and explainability.</p>
      </div>
      <div>
        <h3>Reproducible analytics</h3>
        <p>R, Python, and SQL pipelines built for time-series, visualization, and reporting that holds up under review.</p>
      </div>
      <div>
        <h3>Experiment design</h3>
        <p>Power calculations, randomization, factor design, and the discipline to leave variables you can't control alone.</p>
      </div>
      <div>
        <h3>Data storytelling</h3>
        <p>Translating complicated analyses into visuals and prose people actually retain.</p>
      </div>
      <div>
        <h3>Teaching &amp; curriculum</h3>
        <p>Shiny, webR, and Quarto-based labs and lessons that move students from "I ran the test" to "I can defend the choice."</p>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- Independence -->
<section class="l-section">
  <div class="l-wrap">
    <p class="l-eyebrow">Independence</p>
    <h2>A note on conflicts</h2>
    <p>
      Epsilon Labs is operated entirely outside the principal's primary
      employer. We do not accept engagements that conflict with the
      principal's day-job responsibilities, and we decline work that
      touches the same data systems or stakeholder relationships.
    </p>
  </div>
</section>

</main>
```
